Sighris: Hi! Thanks for your friendly and generous offer to play me again. :)
C[Sighris: Hi! Thanks for your friendly and generous offer to play me again. :)

C[Sighris: My real rank is probably about 8 kyu... The rank OGS shows is a weird error due to a few higher-ranking players who were beating me either timing out or quiting and several other games where I got lucky (many due to my opponent not taking the game as seriously as I did).
C[Sighris: Life is..... what it is..... which for me is mostly good...

C[Sighris: Last year (2023) was rough on me. My father's health declined rapidly, and he died at the end of 2023 - so during 2023 I flew out to Florida from LA 5 or 6 times - a 10+ hour trip each way - plus a loss of about a week (or 2) on all my projects...
Sighris:  (while I was caring for them pretty much 24/7)....
Sighris: After my dad died my mom agreed to move closer to me and live in an ""assisted living"" place - so she has 24/7 access to an on-site nursing staff... So that is good. :)
Sighris: Other than that, most things are good... In particular I have good health, a semi-nice home, plenty of food, and I live in nice-warm LA (I grew up in Chicago).
Sighris: How is life going for you?
Sighris: How is life going for you?
irglbriz: can't really complain, my life has been improving steadily over the last couple years
irglbriz: i'm in the process of starting a side gig
irglbriz: yeah, i wanna see how the first iteration goes before i scout for a second location
C[Sighris: I have been wanting to go to Germany ever since I was a young lad - but the closest I have been so far is Italy.

C[Sighris: What made you decide to try this retreat biz?  & BTW, I wish you success with it... I thought about doing a similiar thing when I was young and single - I thought I could get paid to travel and thus travel more often.
C[irglbriz: yeah i guess it's a bit of a lifestyle business
irglbriz: i really like bjj globetrotters camps
irglbriz: but not everybody trains bjj
C[irglbriz: and i know that a lot of techies like me spend too much time in front of a screen
irglbriz: and miss a bit of real connection to other humans and nature
irglbriz: but to be honest it's just a hunch that there's something there

C[irglbriz: what are you up to professionally these days?
Sighris: Getting a better connection to other humans and nature is a good thing (says the guy who is lacking in both, sadly, lol ). :)
C[Sighris: I've been working only part-time (about 20 - 25+ hours/week) for the last.... IDK, 10+ years....
Sighris:  - except 2020 -  at the beginning of covid....

C[Sighris: It has been good ... My life has has been improving steadily over the last five decades.

C[Sighris: However, I took on too many responisibilities in 2009 - so although my ""professional"" time commitment went down, my time commitment to my parents, to my wife (I got married in 2010 - and emotionally married before then) went way up....

C[Sighris: ...I made some decisions that.... were gambles... that didn't turn out as well as I would have liked.... but also not bad.... so again, give and take.... searching for the right balance....
Sighris: okay, let me think about this move... I should go back to work now....
Sighris: So thanks again - and I wish you good luck with everything.
irglbriz: and i should go to bed
irglbriz: was nice reconnecting with you
C[Sighris: On move #67, when I played F5, I was thinking you would block at E5 so it seemed like a decent enough sente move to me, at the time....
Sighris: ...but now I think a better move would have been something to prevent you from seperating my two right-side groups:
Sighris: And maybe after I threatened to make the cut, and you didn't block, I should have followed through with my threat and cut:
Sighris: I'm not sure yet, but I think if I would have followed through with my cut (instead of just reacting to your move) I could have captured your 6 stones at E4 - C2.

Sighris: BTW, the reason I'm thinking (writing) about this now, instead of at the end of the game, is because there is a four-game tournament coming up here in LA (less than 35 KM east) so I want to thiink a little about my early mid-game as that is where I seem to lose a lot of my games.
Sighris: More often than I like, I forget to look around the whole board to see where the biggest moves might be (often are) located.
Sighris: IF i had made the cut after you played C2 where would the biggest White move be ? Something in the Lower-Left corner or R12 or something else?
Sighris: Can you give me some feedback or thoughts/advice?
C[irglbriz: i tried to resist the cutting threat by playing something first that would solve the problem by living while also making nice endgame points
irglbriz: F6 was probably not necessary and lost sente
irglbriz: but who am i to give advice, you are doing not too bad in the game, eh
irglbriz: good luck on your tournament!
C[Sighris: My opening has improved, I think, but yet I now find it more confusing. My mid game has always been weak, and it is still my weakest play time.
C[Sighris: My opening use to be worse than my mid-game though.... And might still be. lol (Openings and the strange things that happen in corners are still mysterious to me).

Sighris: The end game I can see as just basic math - even if the answer to the formula takes more brain-power than I have available, I pretty much fully grasp it with the one rare exception of  Ihave not studied much the value of seki, but since that is rare in my games I decided to, for now, focus on my openings and mid-game.  I have lost many games early in the game. :(
C[irglbriz: it feels to me most even games below something like 5dan get decided in middle game
irglbriz: and you can probably get to around 1k/1d just by playing good shape and looking at the big picture
irglbriz: then you gotta get precise, either in counting or in reading

C[Sighris: Yes, I agree, most even games (between 12k & 5D-ish) get decided in middle game... but also from my expereince many times I've played even games with people who are better than me, at least in the opening, and when I reviewed the game I discovered I had pretty much lost the game in the opening AND I was totally oblivious. Not until things progressed many moves did I realize my opponet had a large area that I could no longer invade, at least not at my middle-game skill...
Sighris: So I worked on opening moves and I've improved and now I remind myself to invade before it is too late.

Sighris: A recent teacher I had pointed out to me I was too concerned about securing corner territory (a habit I got from misunderstanding a few lessons I got from Yulin Yang!)  when I should have been paying attention to the center territory.
C[irglbriz: usually there is not much territory in the center unless someone is asleep at the wheel
irglbriz: how did your tournament go?
C[Sighris: Overal very good.  They were hoping to get 70 players, and we had about 100, including several 7-Dan players I believe I heard someone mention.

Sighris:  Brian Whitt, a beginner who recently (a year ago?) joined the ""Go Players on FB"" group got a picture taken of him, Chris Hayashida (one of the more active FB group admins), and myself and posted it with a few other photos to the group... It was nice to meet him and see Chris again who I had not seen since the Cotsen 2019 Tournament.
Sighris: Almost all of the players I had not seen since then with the exception of two guys who I had meet up with after the CoViD pandemic was not so bad.
Sighris: There had been several tournaments in the last year but due to me being in Florida a lot (and one being held slightly too far south for me to drive there) I missed all of them.
Sighris: And over the last few years, I think about 90% of my games were slow correspondence games, so my faster playing game skills are rusty, and never were good anyways.

Sighris: I lost my first game that I was winning by a comfortable margin until the last 8 or 10 moves of the game when we were filling in Dame with 10 seconds per move left on our clocks and when I filled in a dama I created an utte-gaeshi situation for myself very similiar to Dia. 7 here: https://senseis.xmp.net/?Snapback#diag7 except I had a two point eye instead of three, with nothing in it... Under the time pressure I failed to see I needed to connect, and my opponent saw it immediately (they probably saw it as, or before, we started filling in the dame and  was just waiting for me to make the mistake. So they won because they were a better player than me. They had almost captured my invasion earlier (which was similiar to my K3-G3-J5 invasion in this game, but I didn't play G2 and instead ran toward the center sooner because I also didn't have a stone at H16 and I was more worried about being cut off).
Sighris: *when I filled in a _dame_ I created an utte-gaeshi situation for myself... (typo correction)

Sighris: I ended up winning two and losing two. So although I was mad at myself at first for not paying attention to something important, I guess I did okay overall, but not good.  ... But I had fun, so I had a good time (two days ago, on Saturday).
C[Sighris: Thanks for asking.  How often do you play in face-to-face ""real life"" Go Tournaments?
C[irglbriz: you win some, you loose some
irglbriz: i don't play on tournaments often, but i play casual games face-to-face every week

C[Sighris: Nice... I had actually meant: How often do you get to play face-to-face/IRL Go gamess? - So thanks for letting me know. I must have had ""tournament on the mind"" so didn't write my question accurately/fully.

Sighris: All of the Go clubs around me are about a 45 minute drive away, and my schedule has not allowed me to go, so most of my F2F Go games have been in tournaments. I do my best to make it to as many of tournaments as I can, so I can see old friends and get in a few rated games.
Sighris: Sadly, the Cotsen Go tournament, which was free to attend, plus free lunch, has not happened since 2019 (since CoViD-19).

Sighris: Hopefully I can study it and it will help me in my next face-to-face tournament here in LA in less than two weeks.
